name: Clytemnestra
description: Clytemnestra, daughter of King Tyndareus of Sparta and Leda, was the wife of Agamemnon, King of Mycenae, and sister to Helen of Troy. Her life was marked by tragedy and vengeance. Agamemnon sacrificed their daughter, Iphigenia, to appease Artemis and ensure favorable winds for the Trojan War. This act fueled Clytemnestra's burning hatred for her husband. While Agamemnon was away at war for ten years, she took Aegisthus, Agamemnon's cousin, as her lover. Together, they plotted Agamemnon's murder upon his return. Clytemnestra, a powerful and determined woman, carried out the assassination, killing both Agamemnon and Cassandra, the Trojan princess he brought home as a concubine. She ruled Mycenae with Aegisthus until their deaths at the hands of Orestes, her son, who avenged his father's murder, fulfilling Apollo's command. Clytemnestra represents a complex figure of betrayal, maternal grief, and righteous anger, challenging traditional notions of female passivity in Greek mythology.
subtitle: Queen of Mycenae and vengeful wife.
achievements: Maintained power during the decade of Agamemnon's absence

keyMyths.title: Sacrifice of Iphigenia
keyMyths.description: Agamemnon sacrificed his daughter Iphigenia to appease Artemis, enraging Clytemnestra and setting the stage for her revenge. This event became the catalyst for her hatred and eventual murder of Agamemnon.

keyMyths.title: Murder of Agamemnon
keyMyths.description: Upon Agamemnon's return from Troy, Clytemnestra, with the help of Aegisthus, murdered him in his bath, avenging Iphigenia's death and seizing control of Mycenae. This act solidified her reputation as a powerful and vengeful queen.

keyMyths.title: Orestes' Revenge
keyMyths.description: Years later, Orestes, Agamemnon's son, returned to Mycenae and, driven by Apollo's command, killed Clytemnestra and Aegisthus to avenge his father's murder. This act completed the cycle of violence within the House of Atreus.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: A tragedy detailing Agamemnon's return and murder.
sources.text: A tragedy detailing Agamemnon's return and murder.
